options.name: Teams

options.name: Messages
options.description: Messages are the individual messages sent in a thread.

options.name: Members
options.description: Members are Inbox users who can access the team.

options.name: Statuses

options.name: Threads Thread Messages Message Reactions Remove

options.description: Remove a reaction from a message. Only works for non-sandbox threads.

options.name: Threads Messages Quick Send

options.name: Tags List

nodeDescription.name: @crowdin/n8n-nodes-preview-crowdin.crowdinFileBased
nodeDescription.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ": " + $parameter["resource"]}}

options.name: Storage
options.description:
Storage is a separate container for each file. You need to use _Add Storage_ method before adding files to your projects via API. Files that should be uploaded into storage include files for localization, screenshots, Glossaries, and Translation Memories.

_Storage id_ is the identifier of the file uploaded to the Storage.

__Note:__ Files uploaded to the storage are kept during the next 24 hours.

options.name: Languages
options.description:
Crowdin supports more than 300 world languages and custom languages created in the system.

Use API to get the list of all supported languages and retrieve additional details (e.g. text direction, internal code) on specific language.

options.name: Projects
options.description:
Using projects, you can keep your source files sorted.

Use API to manage projects, change their settings, or remove them if required.

options.name: Source Files
options.description:
Source files are resources for translation. You can keep files structure using folders or manage different versions of the content via branches.

Use API to keep the source files up to date, check on file revisions, and manage project branches. Before adding source files to the project, upload each file to the Storage first.

__Note:__ If you use branches, make sure your master branch is the first one you integrate with Crowdin.

options.name: Translations
options.description:
Translators can work with entirely untranslated project or you can pre-translate the files to ease the translations process.

Use API to pre-translate files via Machine Translation (MT) or Translation Memory (TM), upload your existing translations, and download translations correspondingly. Pre-translate and build are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

__Note:__ If there are no new translations or changes in build parameters, Crowdin will return the current build for such requests.

options.name: Source Strings
options.description:
Source strings are the text units for translation. Instead of modifying source files, you can manage source strings one by one.

Use API to add, edit, or delete some specific strings  in the source-based and files-based projects (available only for the following file formats: CSV, RESX, JSON, Android XML, iOS strings, PROPERTIES, XLIFF).

options.name: String/Asset Translations
options.description: Use API to add or remove strings/asset translations, approvals, and votes.

options.name: String/Asset Comments
options.description: Use API to list, add, edit or remove string/asset comments.

options.name: Screenshots
options.description:
Screenshots provide translators with additional context for the source strings.  Screenshot tags allow specifying  which source strings are displayed on each screenshot.

Use API to manage screenshots and their tags.

options.name: Glossaries
options.description:
Glossaries help to explain some specific terms or the ones often used in the project so that they can be properly and consistently translated.

Use API to manage glossaries or specific terms. Glossary export and import are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

options.name: Style Guides
options.description:
Style Guides help to provide additional context for the translators.

Use API to manage style guides.

options.name: Translation Memory
options.description:
Translation Memory (TM) is a vault of translations that were previously made in other projects. Those translations can be reused to speed up the translation process. Every translation made in the project is automatically added to the project Translation Memory.

Use API to create, upload, download, or remove specific TM. Translation Memory export and import are [asynchronous operations](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with sequence of API methods.

options.name: Machine Translation Engines
options.description:
Machine Translation Engines (MTE) are the sources for pre-translations. You can currently connect Google Translate, Microsoft Translator, Crowdin Translate, DeepL Pro and Amazon Translate engines.

Use API to add, update, and delete specific MTE.

options.name: Translation Status
options.description:
Status represents the general localization progress on both translations and proofreading.

Use API to check translation and proofreading progress on different levels: file, language, branch, directory.

options.name: Reports
options.description:
Reports help to estimate costs, calculate translation costs, and identify the top members.

Use API to generate Cost Estimate, Translation Cost, and Top Members reports. You can then export reports in .xlsx or .csv file formats. Report generation is an [asynchronous operation](#section/Introduction/Asynchronous-Operations) and shall be completed with a sequence of API methods.

options.name: Tasks
options.description:
Create and assign tasks to get files translated or proofread by specific people. You can set the due dates, split words between people, and receive notifications about the changes and updates on tasks. Tasks are project-specific, so you’ll have to create them within a project.

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific tasks.

options.name: Users
options.description: Users API gives you the possibility to get profile information about the currently authenticated user.

options.name: Webhooks
options.description:
Webhooks allow you to collect information about events that happen in your Crowdin projects. You can select the request type, content type, and add a custom payload, which allows you to create integrations with other systems on your own.

You can configure webhooks for the following events:
 * project file is fully translated
 * project file is fully reviewed
 * project file is fully QA checked
 * all strings in project are translated
 * all strings in project are reviewed
 * all strings in project QA check are finished
 * final translation of string is updated (using Replace in suggestions feature)
 * source string is added
 * source string is updated
 * source string is deleted
 * source string is translated
 * translation for source string is updated (using Replace in suggestions feature)
 * one of translations is deleted
 * translation for string is approved
 * approval for previously added translation is removed

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific webhooks.

options.name: Organization Webhooks
options.description:
Webhooks allow you to collect information about events that happen in your Crowdin account. You can select the request type, content type, and add a custom payload, which allows you to create integrations with other systems on your own.

You can configure webhooks for the following events:
 * project is created
 * project is deleted

Use API to create, modify, and delete specific webhooks.

options.name: Dictionaries
options.description:
Dictionaries allow you to create a storage of words that should be skipped by the spell checker.

Use API to get the list of organization dictionaries and to edit a specific dictionary.

options.name: Applications
options.description:
Crowdin Apps are web applications that can be integrated with Crowdin to extend its functionality.

Use the API to manage the necessary app data.

options.name: Integrations
options.description:
Integration applications provide synchronization between Crowdin and external content management systems.

Use the API to manage integration files, jobs, settings, and authentication.

options.name: Download Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation
options.action: Download Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation

options.name: Reset Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation
options.action: Reset Project File Format Settings Custom Segmentation

options.name: Update or Restore File
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.file` (Read and Write).

Update your current file with a new one or restore it to one of the previous revisions

options.name: List Asset References
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.file` (Read only).

List all reference files for an asset

options.name: Add Asset Reference
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.file` (Read and Write).

Upload a reference file for an asset

options.name: Get Asset Reference
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.file` (Read only).

Get information about a specific asset reference

options.name: Delete Asset Reference
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.file` (Read and Write).

Delete a reference file for an asset

options.name: Export Project Translation
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: List Translation Approvals
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ Either `translationId` OR `fileId` OR `labelIds` OR `excludeLabelIds` with `languageId` OR `stringId` with `languageId` are required

options.name: Remove String/Asset Approvals
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read and Write).

Remove all approvals for a string or asset. Use the oneOf pattern:
- **String type**: Use `stringId`
- **Asset type**: Use `fileId`

options.name: List Language Translations
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: List Translations
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ For instant translation delivery to your mobile, web, server, or desktop apps, it is recommended to use [OTA](https://support.crowdin.com/content-delivery/).

options.name: Delete String/Asset Translations
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read and Write).

Delete translations for a string or an asset. Use the oneOf pattern:
 - **String type**: Use `stringId`
 - **Asset type**: Use `fileId`

options.name: List Translation Votes
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.translation` (Read only).

__Note:__ Either `translationId` OR `fileId` OR `labelIds` OR `excludeLabelIds` with `languageId` OR `stringId` with `languageId` are required

options.name: List String/Asset Comments
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.string` (Read only).

List comments and issues for strings or assets. Use the oneOf pattern:
- **String type**: Use `stringId`
- **Asset type**: Use `fileId`
- **All comments**: Omit both parameters to list all project comments

options.name: Add String/Asset Comment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.source.string` (Read and Write).

Add a comment or issue to a string or asset. Use the oneOf pattern:
- **String type**: Use `stringId`
- **Asset type**: Use `fileId`

options.name: Update concept
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `glossary` (Read and Write).

__Note:__ There is no separate method for concept creation. Concepts are automatically created when adding a term without specifying a `conceptId`.

options.name: Add Term
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `glossary` (Read and Write).

__Note:__ If `conceptId` is not specified, a new concept will be automatically created for the term.

options.name: List TM Segments
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read only).

Get list of translation unit segments

options.name: Create TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Create translation unit with given segments

options.name: Get TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read only).

Get segments of translation unit

options.name: Delete TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Delete all segments of translation unit

options.name: Edit TM Segment
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `tm` (Read and Write).

Edit segment of translation unit

options.name: Revalidate QA Checks
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Triggers revalidation of all QA checks for the project

options.name: QA Checks Revalidation Status
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Returns the status of QA checks revalidation job

options.name: Cancel QA Checks Revalidation
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.status.qa-check` (Read and Write).

Cancels the QA checks revalidation job

options.name: List Tasks
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `project.task` (Read only).

Lists all of the user's project tasks.

options.name: Get Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read only).

Retrieves data from the specified application.

__Note__: Query parameters are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Update or Restore Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Updates or restores data in the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Add Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Adds new data to the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Delete Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Deletes data from the specified application.

__Note__: Query parameters are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

options.name: Edit Application Data
options.description:
**Required scopes:** `application` (Read and Write).

Edits existing data in the specified application.

__Note__: Both the query parameters and request body are application-specific and vary depending on the application being accessed.

nodeDescription.name: n8n-nodes-preview-matrix42.matrix42
nodeDescription.subtitle: ={{$parameter["operation"] + ": " + $parameter["resource"]}}

options.name: Get Fragments
options.description: Receives list of fragments with defined list of columns, which match the specified search criteria

options.name: Add Fragment
options.description: Creates a new Data Definition fragment. The operation is required for cases of multi-fragments or optional fragments.

options.name: Update Fragment
options.description: Updates the specified Data Definition fragment, which has been at first retrieved by the Get Fragment operation, and adjusted with the new data. The Server throws the Concurrency exception in case the updated fragment has been updated between Get Fragment operation and Update Fragment.

options.name: Delete Fragment
options.description: Deletes the fragment from Database defined by the Data Definition name and the fragment ID. The operation is required for cases of multi-fragments or optional fragments.

options.name: Add Object
options.description: Add the Object of the specified Configuration Item

options.name: Get Object
options.description: Reads the whole Object with the specified Configuration Item name and object ID. The Service returns exclusively the data belonged to object (e.g. attributes, N:1 relations) Virtual attributes, like N:M relations are not returned and need to be obtained with the dedicated service request.

options.name: Update Object
options.description: Updates the object of the specified Configuration Item name and object ID. The method works in pair with “Get Object” method, which delivers the original Object with the current Timestamp (market of current state), which is used for concurrency tracking.

options.name: Delete Object
options.description: Deletes the object from Database defined by the Configuration Item name and the object ID
